user_message: Working on Humongous Insurance's territory planning -- need you to run the hygiene check.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-20 13:17 EST
Subject: Re: territory planning Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about territory planning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-01-19 12:12 EST
Subject: Re: Re: territory planning Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about territory planning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Humongous Insurance
Date: 2024-01-07
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance), David Chen (Microsoft), Michelle (Humongous Insurance)

[00:01:30] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into territory planning.

[00:08:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:10:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:13:00] Michelle (Humongous Insurance): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:22:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:25:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:29:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:34:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:39:00] Michelle (Humongous Insurance): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:41:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-M trap (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-M failure mode (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-M -- Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
